title: Filter Inverse using "NOT"?
excerpt: Is there a way to filter items using “NOT” or some similar expression to filter inversely? For example, if a particular Person column has 3 people: Person A, Person B, and Person C;  is there a way to quickly select ever…

posts.author: robzinn (Rob Zinn)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there a way to filter items using “NOT” or some similar expression to filter inversely? For example, if a particular Person column has 3 people: Person A, Person B, and Person C; is there a way to quickly select everyone except for Person A?

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @robzinn thanks for your question! At the moment, there is no specific way to do this in the platform, though I do see how it could be useful.

Would you mind telling me more about your workflow and how this would impact it?

I’d love to pass this along!

Cheers,
Brett

posts.author: hdurey (Hunt Durey)
posts.cookedHtml: Using the filter by everything function on a board (or other places like dashboards where it’s available), if you Shift-click an item in the filter selections window, it will do what you want - inverse filter. In your example, if you Shift-click on Person A, the filter results will show all items that do not include Person A. See the [Select all options “except”] section of this board filters support page https://support.monday.com/hc/en-us/articles/360003624660-The-board-filters?abcb=40269. You can also do this for multiple “Not” selections in the filter.

posts.author: robzinn (Rob Zinn)
posts.cookedHtml: YES! That is what I needed. So great that Monday thought of it! Too bad same question posed to support returned “you can’t do that”.
